name: Patterson 2005 Tadalafil CAD Exercise Ischemia Extract
population_or_context: Older adults with stable coronary artery disease undergoing exercise treadmill testing.
methods: Randomized double-blind two-period crossover trial of tadalafil 10 mg versus placebo on exercise treadmill-induced ischemia and nitroglycerin hemodynamics.
population_profile: Stable CAD adults older than the target population; safety-relevant for exertion and cardiovascular disease.
tadalafil_exposure: Single tadalafil 10 mg dose before treadmill testing and nitroglycerin challenge.
indication_context: Exercise safety and cardiovascular pharmacology in stable CAD.
safety_findings: Tadalafil lowered systolic blood pressure at rest and during/after exercise, and nitroglycerin produced greater hypotensive responses; nitrate coadministration is contraindicated.
directness_to_target_population: Low for healthy performance, but important for men with occult or known CAD and exertion contexts.
confounding_and_bias_limits: CAD sample, older age, small crossover design, ischemia safety endpoint rather than performance enhancement.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence for exertional CAD safety and nitrate boundary, not evidence of exercise enhancement.
